perturbation.original_text: b. On the last day of each month, the refund of Go Call's portion of the deposit shall be calculated by PageMaster Corporation for the &bbsp; prior month and will be forwarded to Go Call by check, along with an extended accounting of all pagers and customers until September 1, 2000, unless otherwise instructed by Go Call.
perturbation.changed_text: b. On the last day of each month, the refund of Go Call's portion of the deposit shall be calculated by PageMaster Corporation for the &bbsp; prior month and will be forwarded to Go Call by check, along with an extended accounting of all pagers and customers. This refund process will continue indefinitely until PageMaster Corporation provides a written notice of termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the refund schedule. Originally, the refund process was set to end on September 1, 2000. Now, the perturbed text states that it continues indefinitely unless PageMaster Corporation provides written notice of termination. This contradicts the original, fixed end date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the execution of this Agreement, Go Call shall forward to PageMaster Corporation a deposit in the sum of $100,000.00 to secure the availability of 100,000 pagers to all Purchase Customers who prepay their annual airtime for this promotion. The deposit is non-refundable except as follows: a. PageMaster Corporation shall refund to Go Call, $1.00 per pager on all pagers delivered to Purchase Customers pursuant to this promotion (net return) up to the maximum refund of $100,000.00. b. On the last day of each month, the refund of Go Call's portion of the deposit shall be calculated by PageMaster Corporation for the &bbsp; prior month and will be forwarded to Go Call by check, along with an extended accounting of all pagers and customers. This refund process will continue indefinitely until PageMaster Corporation provides a written notice of termination. Go Call, upon ten (10) days written notice, shall have the right to examine the books and records of PageMaster Corporation to verify the sales resulting from this promotion. Such examination shall be made at the regular place of business of PageMaster Corporation where such books and records are maintained during normal business hours and shall be conducted at Go Call's expense by a certified public accountant or other Go Call executive so designated by Go Call.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a termination date of September 1, 2000, for the refund process. The changed text removes this specific date and replaces it with an indefinite continuation of the refund process until a written notice of termination is provided by PageMaster Corporation. This directly contradicts the original, fixed termination date, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction, as the refund process cannot simultaneously end on September 1, 2000, and continue indefinitely until notice is given. The Contradicted Text contains the changed text, and it directly alters the original agreement, therefore a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This promotion shall begin on June 1,1999 and shall terminate June 1, 2000 (herein "Term") This term shall be extended for a 1 year period provided 3000 pagers per month are distributed to Purchase customers.
perturbation.changed_text: This promotion shall begin on June 1,1999 and shall terminate June 1, 2000 (herein "Term") This term shall be extended for a 1 year period provided 3000 pagers per month are distributed to Purchase customers. However, PageMaster Corporation has the right to terminate this agreement with a 30-day written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the termination of the promotion. The original text allows a one-year extension based on pager distribution. The added sentence gives PageMaster Corporation the unilateral right to terminate with 30 days' notice, regardless of pager distribution, creating conflict on the termination conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This promotion shall begin on June 1,1999 and shall terminate June 1, 2000 (herein "Term") This term shall be extended for a 1 year period provided 3000 pagers per month are distributed to Purchase customers. However, PageMaster Corporation has the right to terminate this agreement with a 30-day written notice.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the term is extended based on pager distribution. The changed text adds a clause allowing PageMaster Corporation to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice, regardless of the pager distribution. This creates a direct contradiction, as the term could be extended based on pager distribution, but also terminated by PageMaster with 30 days' notice, making the conditions of extension and termination inconsistent and therefore contradictory, which is a violation of the "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction" definition.

perturbation.original_text: PageMaster Corporation will pay Go Call $3.00 per pager (beginning with pager # 1) and 5% of all airtime renewal revenue for each pager redeemed for this promotion consistent with the terms of paragraph 6b of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: PageMaster Corporation will pay Go Call $3.00 per pager (beginning with pager # 1) for the first year and 5% of all airtime renewal revenue for each pager redeemed for this promotion consistent with the terms of paragraph 6b of this Agreement. After the first year, PageMaster will only pay $1.50 per pager.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction on the payment amount for each pager. The original text establishes a fixed payment of $3.00 per pager. Now the text specifies that after the first year it will only be $1.50 per pager. This contradicts the original flat rate leading to uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PageMaster Corporation shall pay to Go Call, Co-Op Marketing funds for the promotion. PageMaster Corporation will pay Go Call $3.00 per pager (beginning with pager # 1) for the first year and 5% of all airtime renewal revenue for each pager redeemed for this promotion consistent with the terms of paragraph 6b of this Agreement. After the first year, PageMaster will only pay $1.50 per pager.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment amount per pager from a fixed $3.00 to $3.00 for the first year and $1.50 thereafter. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, reflects this new tiered payment structure. This creates a contradiction if other parts of the agreement still refer to a flat $3.00 payment as the only payment option. The explanation provided explicitly states that the change introduces an in-text contradiction by creating a variable payment amount where previously there was a fixed rate. This inconsistency falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of the contract now provide conflicting obligations regarding payment.
